Okay, so now that you've earned your prospects attention with the pre-Headline qualifier and implied either an injustice or a promise or both, it's time to expand that out with what I call the Question Loop Headline. Now the main goal of the Question Loop Headline is to really set the gap and ultimately invite both resourcefulness and curiosity on how to bridge it. So the main tool we're going to be using here is the single most important question. Now this is not only a question that will clearly set the gap, but one question that when unpacked through your sales message and solved through your product will literally change your prospects life. This question literally sets the foundation for the rest of your sales letter. So it's where I'm going to invite you to spend some time to get it right. Now I like to call this the Rule of One with a coaching twist. So David Ogilvy introduced the concept of one big idea or the Rule of One which states that we need to focus only on one idea. Likewise, your entire sales message or funnel should be built around one question which if solved changes everything for your prospect in the context of the problem. And since asking powerful questions is the foundation for being an effective coach, we're going to use a question to invite that resourcefulness and invite our prospects to explore it with us. So the question sets the gap. It's an invitation for your prospect to explore that gap and it invites resourcefulness and gets them in that solution focused state of receptivity. And since there's so much at stake and getting it answered, reading our sales page becomes necessary to close that question loop. Now before this gets like way too heady, I really want to make this practical. So as a quick overview, here is what you'll be coaching with your question loop headline. You're going to be coaching curiosity around the question, resourcefulness in gathering the energy to have it answered, excitement by returning to that implied promise and through that one question alone having awareness of that gap. So it's important to note that curiosity will only exist if the answer to the question is a value to them. You know, so many people today misuse the concept of open loops by giving some irrelevant or unimportant detail like I did something crazy this weekend, but before I tell you what that is and that is an open loop in the purest sense. It's just not that exciting. It needs to be clear. It needs to have that what's in it for me and why does this matter to me value within that loop. So that's why it's important for your question to have an exciting outcome or promise embedded within it. And with all that said, let's dive into the next slide where I will show you how to write it. So version one is the if without would. So it goes like this, if there was a way to achieve big promise without process related pain points, would you finally feel confident committing to achieving X. Now let's see this in action. So going back to Amy's list builders example, this was our question loop headline. If there was a predictable step by step method for attracting a steady surge of perfectly targeted leads every single day without the confusion, tech overwhelm or crippling what the heck do I do next syndrome? Would you finally feel confident enough to make profitable list building your businesses? Number one priority. So once again, we're creating that gap. We have the starting point, you know, we're implying that list building is not their number one priority that they're having all these process related pain points that is stalling them. And we are taking them to the other end of the spectrum with implied promise of a steady surge of perfectly targeted leads every single day. So that was one example. The other version of this is the difference maker. Now the difference maker goes like this. What's the only or biggest difference between people who achieve big promise and those who experience the moment of highest tension? So let's see how this one plays out. So this was from our 10 X launches program. What's the biggest difference between a game changing product launch and one that barely covers your infusion soft bill? Now this is a program that goes on to sell training on launch emails yet what is absent from this headline? Launch emails. We're really establishing the gap between where they are, you know, barely being able to cover an infusion soft bill and where they want to be in this case a game changing product launch. Now for a slightly more colorful example of this difference maker, difference maker region we have funnels that scale by my friend Greg Hickman. So he writes, what's the only difference between a powerful automated sales funnel that allows you to confidently scale up to 450k per year and one that you ugly cry about in your masterminds threatened to fire team members over and feel like a total failure as your financial runway gets shorter than a Seth Godin blog post. So what I want to point out here is not only the elements of the question loop headline but the language that really brings it to life, you know, Seth Godin blog post, cry about in your masterminds threatened to fire team members over. These are those intimate details that were extracted by going through the 67 questions and that's why especially the ethos graphics and demographic data and yeah, psychographics those are going to get infused in literally every part of your sales letter. So that's how you bring your copy to life. Final example, let's go into indestructible team by soul powered. So they write, what's the biggest difference between predictable stress free stress free growth powered by a committed team of highly engaged A players. That's the promise and a stagnating bottom line crumbling under the bloated overhead of underperforming team members ready to jump ship at the first sign of struggle. So with those examples in mind, I invite you to take a second, see which version of this works better for your product and then write your question loop headline. I'll see you in the next lesson.
